summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording of a legendary Englishtown show with scorching Peggy‑O, Eyes of the World and a monster He's Gone → Not Fade Away → Truckin finish.
review: Fans rave about this 1977 Englishtown performance, calling it one of the best shows of the year. Standout moments include an epic Peggy‑O, fiery Eyes of the World, a soaring Loser solo and a monster He's Gone → Not Fade Away → Truckin sequence that ends with a powerful Terrapin encore. The audience recording is praised as crisp, clear and powerful – essentially an excellent capture of the night’s energy.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (average ~4.95★) praise both the musical performance and the audio quality; most reviewers award five stars and highlight numerous standout songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear and powerful sound; several reviewers call it the best audience recording of this show. No notable drop‑outs or muffling reported.

standout_songs: He's Gone > Not Fade Away > Truckin
show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets flow with high energy; transitions such as He's Gone into Not Fade Away are highlighted. Reviewers describe the performance as crisp, consistent and bursting with gusto.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solos receive universal praise – Loser solo described as "simply amazing", multiple jam highlights on Eyes of the World, and overall guitar action called "ridiculous".
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer notes "sweat dripping from Bob" during his playing, indicating intense performance.
